name: search_code
description:
Searches for code patterns across files in a local directory or a remote GitHub repository. Local mode requires no token; GitHub mode uses the GitHub connector (optional for public repos).
Use when: user wants to find where a function, class, variable, or pattern is used across a codebase — locally or on GitHub.
Scope: local directory walk (no token) OR GitHub code search API (token optional for public repos).
Not for: fetching a specific known file (use get_file), analyzing commit history (use analyze_commits), or finding security issues (use repo_scanner).

pattern.description: Search pattern — regex for local mode, keyword/qualifier for GitHub mode. Example: 'def run\(' or 'PyGithub language:python'

path.description: Local directory to search. Use this for local mode. Omit if using 'repo'.

repo.description: GitHub repo in 'owner/repo' format. Use this for GitHub mode. Omit if using 'path'. Example: 'agenticstore/agentic-store-mcp'

file_extension.description: Filter by file extension. Example: 'py', 'ts', 'go'. Optional.

max_results.description: Maximum number of results to return. Default: 50.
